question: How should Blue implement a local development environment that achieves full production parity—including DynamoDB with E2E encryption, Infisical secrets management, and the visualization dashboard—while maintaining developer ergonomics and ensuring encryption code paths are exercised identically to production?

experts.focus: Docker orchestration, service composition, local stack architecture
Encryption mode boundaries, key management tiers, zero-knowledge preservation
Developer onboarding, friction reduction, npm-start simplicity
AWS service parity, DynamoDB Local, LocalStack, API Gateway mocking
Dashboard decryption UI, encrypted/plaintext toggle, real-time updates
DynamoDB Local configuration, schema parity, encryption at rest
Infisical local mode, dotenv fallback, secret rotation simulation
Key derivation parity, algorithm consistency, mode detection
Observability, debugging encrypted systems, metrics collection
Docker networking, volume mounts, port mapping
Integration tests, encryption path validation, parity verification
Setup documentation, troubleshooting guides, architecture diagrams
API routes, storage abstraction layer, middleware
Zero-knowledge principles, what must never be visible even locally
Audit trail requirements, logging in dev environments

Failure modes, key unavailability, graceful degradation
